tldr: Headlines universally report Mangione's lawyers dropping the psychiatric defense, but split on what to foreground: Western outlets lead with the legal mechanism (psychiatry, defense withdrawal), while wire-adjacent and Asian/African press lead with Mangione's criminal identity as 'accused CEO killer.'

evidence.quote: won't assert a psychiatric defence in state trial
won't pursue psychiatric defence in stunning reversal
desiste de alegação psiquiátrica em julgamento
lawyers withdraw psychiatric defense in CEO murder trial
Mangione, accused CEO killer, withdraws mental health
accused CEO killer, withdraws mental health defense plans
Accused CEO killer Mangione withdraws emotional

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline to use 'stunning reversal' — the sole evaluative intensifier across all nine headlines, matching Canada's exclusive corpus vocab ('stunning', 'reversal').

Only headline to omit any reference to psychiatry or mental health, abstracting the withdrawn defense to a 'Controversial Courtroom Tactic' — the only outlet to apply a political/evaluative label rather than neutral procedural description.
Adds 'for now' qualifier — framing the withdrawal as provisional rather than definitive — a nuance absent from most other headlines.

bottom_line: The headline layer divides cleanly: a LEGALITY frame (Canada, UK, Brazil, Mexico — leading with the psychiatric defense mechanism) vs. a CRIME_PUNISHMENT frame (wire, South Africa, Singapore — leading with 'accused CEO killer'), with the US conservative Daily Wire alone injecting POLITICAL valence by labeling the defense a 'controversial tactic.'
